This module defines the core parser traits that different parsing\n4\t//! strategies (YAML, JSON, etc.) can implement.\n5\t\n6\tuse std::path::Path;\n7\tuse crate::parsers::yaml::ParseError as YamlParseError;\n8\t\n9\t/// Generic parser trait for different parsing strategies\n10\t///\n11\t/// This trait defines the core interface for parsers that transform\n12\t/// input of one type into structured output. It is designed to be:\n13\t///\n14\t/// 1. **Generic** - Works with any input/output type combination\n15\t/// 2. **Composable** - Multiple parsers can be chained together\n16\t/// 3. **Extensible** - Supports streaming and incremental parsing\n17\t/// 4. **Error-aware** - Uses structured error types for consistent error handling\n18\t///\n19\t/// # Type Parameters\n20\t///\n21\t/// * `Input` - The input source type (e.g., `&str`, `&[u8]`, `&Path`)\n22\t/// * `Output` - The parsed result type (e.g., configuration struct, AST)\n23\t///\n24\t/// # Trait Bounds\n25\t///\n26\t/// For common use cases, the following type combinations are typical:\n27\t///\n28\t/// - `Input = &str` for string-based formats (YAML, JSON, TOML)\n29\t/// - `Input = &[u8]` for binary formats\n30\t/// - `Input = &Path` for file-based parsing (delegates to string parsing)\n31\t///\n32\t/// The `Output` type is the target representation after parsing.\n33\t///\n34\t/// # Core Method\n35\t///\n36\t/// The [`Parser::parse()`] method is the core parsing operation:\n37\t///\n38\t/// ```ignore\n39\t/// fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n40\t/// ```\n41\t///\n42\t/// # Examples\n43\t///\n44\t/// ## Basic String Parsing\n45\t///\n46\t/// ```ignore\n47\t/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n48\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n49\t///\n50\t/// struct ConfigParser;\n51\t///\n52\t/// struct Config {\n53\t///     name: String,\n54\t///     value: i32,\n55\t/// }\n56\t///\n57\t/// impl Parser<&str, Config> for ConfigParser {\n58\t///     fn parse(&self, source: &str) -> Result<Config, ParseError> {\n59\t///         // Parse logic here\n60\t///         Ok(Config { name: \"test\".to_string(), value: 42 })\n61\t///     }\n62\t/// }\n63\t///\n64\t/// let parser = ConfigParser;\n65\t/// let config = parser.parse(\"name: test\\nvalue: 42\")?;\n66\t/// ```\n67\t///\n68\t/// ## Chaining Parsers\n69\t///\n70\t/// ```ignore\n71\t/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n72\t///\n73\t/// // Parser 1: Raw string -> Intermediate representation\n74\t/// struct FirstParser;\n75\t/// impl Parser<&str, Intermediate> for FirstParser { /* ... */ }\n76\t///\n77\t/// // Parser 2: Intermediate -> Final output\n78\t/// struct SecondParser;\n79\t/// impl Parser<Intermediate, Output> for SecondParser { /* ... */ }\n80\t///\n81\t/// let first = FirstParser;\n82\t/// let second = SecondParser;\n83\t/// let output = second.parse(first.parse(input)?);\n84\t/// ```\n85\t///\n86\t/// ## Error Handling\n87\t///\n88\t/// ```ignore\n89\t/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n90\t///\n91\t/// match parser.parse(source) {\n92\t///     Ok(output) => println!(\"Parsed successfully: {:?}\", output),\n93\t///     Err(ParseError::Syntax(msg)) => eprintln!(\"Syntax error: {}\", msg),\n94\t///     Err(ParseError::Io(msg)) => eprintln!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n95\t///     Err(e) => eprintln!(\"Other error: {}\", e),\n96\t/// }\n97\t/// ```\n98\tpub trait Parser<Input, Output> {\n99\t    /// Parse input and return the parsed result\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// This method accepts input of type `Input` and attempts to parse it\n102\t    /// into a structured `Output` type. Returns `Ok(Output)` on success,\n103\t    /// or `Err(ParseError)` if parsing fails.\n104\t    ///\n105\t    /// # Arguments\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// # Returns\n110\t    ///\n111\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n112\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n113\t    ///\n114\t    /// # Errors\n115\t    ///\n116\t    /// This method will return an error if:\n117\t    /// - The input syntax is invalid (syntax error)\n118\t    /// - The input cannot be read (I/O error)\n119\t    /// - The input violates constraints (validation error)\n120\t    /// - Types don't match expectations (type mismatch)\n121\t    ///\n122\t    /// # Examples\n123\t    ///\n124\t    /// ```ignore\n125\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n126\t    ///\n127\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n128\t    /// let result = parser.parse(\"key: value\")?;\n129\t    /// ```\n130\t    fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n131\t\n132\t    /// Parse input with extended options\n133\t    ///\n134\t    /// This method provides a way to customize parsing behavior through\n135\t    /// options. The default implementation delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// # Arguments\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n140\t    /// * `options` - Parsing options to customize behavior\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// # Returns\n143\t    ///\n144\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n145\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// # Examples\n148\t    ///\n149\t    /// ```ignore\n150\t    /// use armor::parsers::{Parser, ParseOptions};\n151\t    ///\n152\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n153\t    /// let options = ParseOptions {\n154\t    ///     strict_mode: true,\n155\t    ///     preserve_comments: false,\n156\t    ///     ..Default::default()\n157\t    /// };\n158\t    /// let result = parser.parse_with_options(source, options)?;\n159\t    /// ```\n160\t    fn parse_with_options(&self, source: Input, options: ParseOptions) -> Result<Output, ParseError> {\n161\t        let _ = options; // Ignore options in default implementation\n162\t        self.parse(source)\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Parse a file at the given path\n166\t    ///\n167\t    /// Convenience method that reads a file and parses its contents.\n168\t    /// The default implementation reads the file and delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n169\t    ///\n170\t    /// # Arguments\n171\t    ///\n172\t    /// * `path` - Path to the file to parse\n173\t    ///\n174\t    /// # Returns\n175\t    ///\n176\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n177\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - I/O or parsing error\n178\t    ///\n179\t    /// # Errors\n180\t    ///\n181\t    /// This method will return an error if:\n182\t    /// - The file cannot be read (I/O error)\n183\t    /// - The file contents are invalid (parsing error)\n184\t    ///\n185\t    /// # Examples\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// ```ignore\n188\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n189\t    /// use std::path::Path;\n190\t    ///\n191\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n192\t    /// let result = parser.parse_file(Path::new(\"config.yaml\"))?;\n193\t    /// ```\n194\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n195\t    where\n196\t        Self: Sized,\n197\t        Input: From<String>,\n198\t    {\n199\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n200\t            .map_err(|e| ParseError::Io(e.to_string()))?;\n201\t        self.parse(content.into())\n202\t    }\n203\t\n204\t    /// Validate input without fully parsing\n205\t    ///\n206\t    /// This method performs lightweight validation to check if the input\n207\t    /// is well-formed without constructing the full output structure.\n208\t    /// The default implementation attempts parsing and discards the result.\n209\t    ///\n210\t    /// # Arguments\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// * `source` - The input source to validate\n213\t    ///\n214\t    /// # Returns\n215\t    ///\n216\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Input is valid\n217\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Input is invalid\n218\t    ///\n219\t    /// # Examples\n220\t    ///\n221\t    /// ```ignore\n222\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n223\t    ///\n224\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n225\t    /// if parser.validate(\"key: value\").is_ok() {\n226\t    ///     println!(\"Input is valid!\");\n227\t    /// }\n228\t    /// ```\n229\t    fn validate(&self, source: Input) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n230\t        self.parse(source)?;\n231\t        Ok(())\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Get metadata about the parser\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// Returns information about the parser's capabilities and configuration.\n237\t    ///\n238\t    /// # Returns\n239\t    ///\n240\t    /// Parser metadata including supported formats and features\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// # Examples\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// ```ignore\n245\t    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n248\t    /// let metadata = parser.metadata();\n249\t    /// println!(\"Parser: {}\", metadata.name());\n250\t    /// println!(\"Supports streaming: {}\", metadata.supports_streaming());\n251\t    /// ```\n252\t    fn metadata(&self) -> ParseMetadata {\n253\t        ParseMetadata::default()\n254\t    }\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\t/// Trait for parsers that support streaming input\n258\t///\n259\t/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with capabilities for processing large\n260\t/// inputs as a stream of chunks or multiple sources.\n261\t///\n262\t/// # Examples\n263\t///\n264\t/// ```ignore\n265\t/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, StreamingParser};\n266\t///\n267\t/// let parser = MyStreamingParser::new();\n268\t///\n269\t/// // Parse multiple sources\n270\t/// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n271\t/// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n272\t///\n273\t/// for result in results {\n274\t///     println!(\"Parsed: {:?}\", result);\n275\t/// }\n276\t/// ```\n277\tpub trait StreamingParser<Input, Output>: Parser<Input, Output> {\n278\t    /// Parse multiple sources in sequence\n279\t    ///\n280\t    /// This method processes a collection of input sources and returns\n281\t    /// a vector of parsed results. Errors from individual sources are\n282\t    /// collected and returned together.\n283\t    ///\n284\t    /// # Arguments\n285\t    ///\n286\t    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n287\t    ///\n288\t    /// # Returns\n289\t    ///\n290\t    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n291\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n292\t    ///\n293\t    /// # Examples\n294\t    ///\n295\t    /// ```ignore\n296\t    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n297\t    ///\n298\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n299\t    /// let sources = vec![\"config1.yaml\", \"config2.yaml\"];\n300\t    /// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n301\t    /// ```\n302\t    fn parse_stream<'a, I>(\n303\t        &self,\n304\t        sources: I,\n305\t    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n306\t    where\n307\t        Input: 'a,\n308\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>;\n309\t\n310\t    /// Parse sources in parallel\n311\t    ///\n312\t    /// This method processes multiple sources concurrently using parallel\n313\t    /// execution. Results are collected in the order of the input iterator.\n314\t    ///\n315\t    /// # Arguments\n316\t    ///\n317\t    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n318\t    /// * `parallelism` - Maximum number of concurrent parse operations\n319\t    ///\n320\t    /// # Returns\n321\t    ///\n322\t    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n323\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n324\t    ///\n325\t    /// # Examples\n326\t    ///\n327\t    /// ```ignore\n328\t    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n329\t    ///\n330\t    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n331\t    /// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n332\t    /// let results = parser.parse_parallel(sources, 4)?;\n333\t    /// ```\n334\t    fn parse_parallel<'a, I>(\n335\t        &self,\n336\t        sources: I,\n337\t        parallelism: usize,\n338\t    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n339\t    where\n340\t        Input: 'a,\n341\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>,\n342\t    {\n343\t        // Default implementation processes sequentially\n344\t        self.parse_stream(sources)\n345\t    }\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t/// Trait for parsers that support incremental parsing\n349\t///\n350\t/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with the ability to parse input in chunks,\n351\t/// allowing processing of large inputs without loading the entire source\n352\t/// into memory.\n353\t///\n354\t/// # Examples\n355\t///\n356\t/// ```ignore\n357\t/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, IncrementalParser};\n358\t///\n359\t/// let parser = MyIncrementalParser::new();\n360\t///\n361\t/// // Initialize parsing\n362\t/// parser.init_parse()?;\n363\t///\n364\t/// // Feed chunks incrementally\n365\t/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"key: \")?;\n366\t/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"value\")?;\n367\t///\n368\t/// // Complete parsing and get result\n369\t/// let result = parser.finalize_parse()?;\n370\t/// ```\n371\tpub trait IncrementalParser<Output>: Parser<Vec<u8>, Output> {\n372\t    /// Initialize incremental parsing\n373\t    ///\n374\t    /// Called before feeding chunks. Sets up internal state for parsing.\n375\t    ///\n376\t    /// # Returns\n377\t    ///\n378\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Parser initialized successfully\n379\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Initialization failed\n380\t    fn init_parse(&mut self) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n381\t\n382\t    /// Feed a chunk of input to the parser\n383\t    ///\n384\t    /// Processes a chunk of input without completing the parse. Can be\n385\t    /// called multiple times before finalization.\n386\t    ///\n387\t    /// # Arguments\n388\t    ///\n389\t    /// * `chunk` - Chunk of input data\n390\t    ///\n391\t    /// # Returns\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Chunk processed successfully\n394\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Chunk processing failed\n395\t    fn feed_chunk(&mut self, chunk: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n396\t\n397\t    /// Finalize parsing and return the result\n398\t    ///\n399\t    /// Called after all chunks have been fed. Completes the parse and\n400\t    /// returns the final result.\n401\t    ///\n402\t    /// # Returns\n403\t    ///\n404\t    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Parsing completed successfully\n405\t    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Finalization or parsing failed\n406\t    fn finalize_parse(&mut self) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n407\t}\n408\t\n409\t/// Options for customizing parser behavior\n410\t///\n411\t/// This structure provides configuration options that can be passed to\n412\t/// [`Parser::parse_with_options()`].\n413\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n414\tpub struct ParseOptions {\n415\t    /// Enable strict mode (disallow unknown fields, enforce types strictly)\n416\t    pub strict_mode: bool,\n417\t    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n418\t    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n419\t    /// Allow duplicate keys (if format normally disallows them)\n420\t    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n421\t    /// Maximum depth for nested structures (0 = no limit)\n422\t    pub max_depth: usize,\n423\t    /// Custom delimiter for key-value pairs (None = use format default)\n424\t    pub delimiter: Option<char>,\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\timpl ParseOptions {\n428\t    /// Create new parse options with default values\n429\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n430\t        Self::default()\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    /// Create options for strict parsing\n434\t    ///\n435\t    /// Sets `strict_mode` to true and `allow_duplicates` to false.\n436\t    ///\n437\t    /// # Examples\n438\t    ///\n439\t    /// ```\n440\t    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n441\t    ///\n442\t    /// let options = ParseOptions::strict();\n443\t    /// assert!(options.strict_mode);\n444\t    /// assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n445\t    /// ```\n446\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n447\t        Self {\n448\t            strict_mode: true,\n449\t            allow_duplicates: false,\n450\t            ..Default::default()\n451\t        }\n452\t    }\n453\t\n454\t    /// Create options for lenient parsing\n455\t    ///\n456\t    /// Sets `strict_mode` to false and `allow_duplicates` to true.\n457\t    ///\n458\t    /// # Examples\n459\t    ///\n460\t    /// ```\n461\t    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n462\t    ///\n463\t    /// let options = ParseOptions::lenient();\n464\t    /// assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n465\t    /// assert!(options.allow_duplicates);\n466\t    /// ```\n467\t    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n468\t        Self {\n469\t            strict_mode: false,\n470\t            allow_duplicates: true,\n471\t            ..Default::default()\n472\t        }\n473\t    }\n474\t\n475\t    /// Builder method: set strict mode\n476\t    pub fn with_strict_mode(mut self, strict: bool) -> Self {\n477\t        self.strict_mode = strict;\n478\t        self\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    /// Builder method: set comment preservation\n482\t    pub fn with_preserve_comments(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n483\t        self.preserve_comments = preserve;\n484\t        self\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    /// Builder method: set duplicate key allowance\n488\t    pub fn with_allow_duplicates(mut self, allow: bool) -> Self {\n489\t        self.allow_duplicates = allow;\n490\t        self\n491\t    }\n492\t\n493\t    /// Builder method: set maximum nesting depth\n494\t    pub fn with_max_depth(mut self, depth: usize) -> Self {\n495\t        self.max_depth = depth;\n496\t        self\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    /// Builder method: set custom delimiter\n500\t    pub fn with_delimiter(mut self, delimiter: char) -> Self {\n501\t        self.delimiter = Some(delimiter);\n502\t        self\n503\t    }\n504\t}\n505\t\n506\t/// Metadata about a parser's capabilities\n507\t///\n508\t/// This structure describes what a parser can do, what formats it supports,\n509\t/// and its current configuration.\n510\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n511\tpub struct ParseMetadata {\n512\t    /// Human-readable name of the parser (e.g., \"YAML Parser\", \"JSON Parser\")\n513\t    pub name: String,\n514\t    /// Format name this parser handles (e.g., \"YAML 1.2\", \"JSON\")\n515\t    pub format: String,\n516\t    /// Whether the parser supports streaming operations\n517\t    pub supports_streaming: bool,\n518\t    /// Whether the parser supports incremental parsing\n519\t    pub supports_incremental: bool,\n520\t    /// Supported file extensions (e.g., [\"yaml\", \"yml\"])\n521\t    pub extensions: Vec<String>,\n522\t    /// Parser version\n523\t    pub version: String,\n524\t}\n525\t\n526\timpl ParseMetadata {\n527\t    /// Create new parser metadata\n528\t    pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>, format: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n529\t        Self {\n530\t            name: name.into(),\n531\t            format: format.into(),\n532\t            supports_streaming: false,\n533\t            supports_incremental: false,\n534\t            extensions: Vec::new(),\n535\t            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n536\t        }\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    /// Builder method: indicate streaming support\n540\t    pub fn with_streaming(mut self, supports: bool) -> Self {\n541\t        self.supports_streaming = supports;\n542\t        self\n543\t    }\n544\t\n545\t    /// Builder method: indicate incremental parsing support\n546\t    pub fn with_incremental(mut self, supports: bool) -> Self {\n547\t        self.supports_incremental = supports;\n548\t        self\n549\t    }\n550\t\n551\t    /// Builder method: add supported file extension\n552\t    pub fn with_extension(mut self, ext: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n553\t        self.extensions.push(ext.into());\n554\t        self\n555\t    }\n556\t\n557\t    /// Builder method: set parser version\n558\t    pub fn with_version(mut self, version: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n559\t        self.version = version.into();\n560\t        self\n561\t    }\n562\t\n563\t    /// Get the parser name\n564\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &str {\n565\t        &self.name\n566\t    }\n567\t\n568\t    /// Get the format name\n569\t    pub fn format(&self) -> &str {\n570\t        &self.format\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    /// Check if streaming is supported\n574\t    pub fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n575\t        self.supports_streaming\n576\t    }\n577\t\n578\t    /// Check if incremental parsing is supported\n579\t    pub fn supports_incremental(&self) -> bool {\n580\t        self.supports_incremental\n581\t    }\n582\t\n583\t    /// Get supported file extensions\n584\t    pub fn extensions(&self) -> &[String] {\n585\t        &self.extensions\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    /// Get parser version\n589\t    pub fn version(&self) -> &str {\n590\t        &self.version\n591\t    }\n592\t}\n593\t\n594\timpl Default for ParseMetadata {\n595\t    fn default() -> Self {\n596\t        Self {\n597\t            name: \"Generic Parser\".to_string(),\n598\t            format: \"Unknown\".to_string(),\n599\t            supports_streaming: false,\n600\t            supports_incremental: false,\n601\t            extensions: Vec::new(),\n602\t            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n603\t        }\n604\t    }\n605\t}\n606\t\n607\t/// Unified parse error type\n608\t///\n609\t/// This type wraps format-specific errors (e.g., `yaml::ParseError`)\n610\t/// into a common type that the generic `Parser` trait can use.\n611\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n612\tpub enum ParseError {\n613\t    /// YAML parsing error\n614\t    Yaml(YamlParseError),\n615\t    /// I/O error\n616\t    Io(String),\n617\t    /// Validation error\n618\t    Validation(String),\n619\t    /// Type mismatch error\n620\t    TypeMismatch {\n621\t        field: String,\n622\t        expected: String,\n623\t        actual: String,\n624\t    },\n625\t    /// Syntax error\n626\t    Syntax(String),\n627\t    /// Other error\n628\t    Other(String),\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\timpl ParseError {\n632\t    /// Create a syntax error\n633\t    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n634\t        Self::Syntax(msg.into())\n635\t    }\n636\t\n637\t    /// Create an I/O error\n638\t    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n639\t        Self::Io(msg.into())\n640\t    }\n641\t\n642\t    /// Create a validation error\n643\t    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n644\t        Self::Validation(msg.into())\n645\t    }\n646\t\n647\t    /// Create a type mismatch error\n648\t    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n649\t        Self::TypeMismatch {\n650\t            field: field.into(),\n651\t            expected: expected.into(),\n652\t            actual: actual.into(),\n653\t        }\n654\t    }\n655\t\n656\t    /// Create an other error\n657\t    pub fn other(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n658\t        Self::Other(msg.into())\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n662\t    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n663\t        matches!(self, Self::Syntax(_))\n664\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_syntax())\n665\t    }\n666\t\n667\t    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n668\t    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n669\t        matches!(self, Self::Io(_))\n670\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_io())\n671\t    }\n672\t\n673\t    /// Check if this is a validation error\n674\t    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n675\t        matches!(self, Self::Validation(_))\n676\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_validation())\n677\t    }\n678\t\n679\t    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n680\t    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n681\t        matches!(self, Self::TypeMismatch { .. })\n682\t            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_type_mismatch())\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    /// Get a brief summary of the error\n686\t    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n687\t        match self {\n688\t            Self::Yaml(e) => e.summary(),\n689\t            Self::Io(msg) => format!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n690\t            Self::Validation(msg) => format!(\"validation error: {}\", msg),\n691\t            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n692\t                format!(\"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n693\t            }\n694\t            Self::Syntax(msg) => format!(\"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n695\t            Self::Other(msg) => format!(\"error: {}\", msg),\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\timpl std::fmt::Display for ParseError {\n701\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n702\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())\n703\t    }\n704\t}\n705\t\n706\timpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n707\t\n708\timpl From<YamlParseError> for ParseError {\n709\t    fn from(err: YamlParseError) -> Self {\n710\t        Self::Yaml(err)\n711\t    }\n712\t}\n713\t\n714\timpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n715\t    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n716\t        Self::Io(err.to_string())\n717\t    }\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t#[cfg(test)]\n721\tmod tests {\n722\t    use Generic parser traits and interfaces\n//!\n//! This module defines the core parser traits that different parsing\n//! strategies (YAML, JSON, etc.) can implement.\n\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::ParseError as YamlParseError;\n\n/// Generic parser trait for different parsing strategies\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsers that transform\n/// input of one type into structured output. It is designed to be:\n///\n/// 1. **Generic** - Works with any input/output type combination\n/// 2. **Composable** - Multiple parsers can be chained together\n/// 3. **Extensible** - Supports streaming and incremental parsing\n/// 4. **Error-aware** - Uses structured error types for consistent error handling\n///\n/// # Type Parameters\n///\n/// * `Input` - The input source type (e.g., `&str`, `&[u8]`, `&Path`)\n/// * `Output` - The parsed result type (e.g., configuration struct, AST)\n///\n/// # Trait Bounds\n///\n/// For common use cases, the following type combinations are typical:\n///\n/// - `Input = &str` for string-based formats (YAML, JSON, TOML)\n/// - `Input = &[u8]` for binary formats\n/// - `Input = &Path` for file-based parsing (delegates to string parsing)\n///\n/// The `Output` type is the target representation after parsing.\n///\n/// # Core Method\n///\n/// The [`Parser::parse()`] method is the core parsing operation:\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n/// ```\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ## Basic String Parsing\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\n///\n/// struct ConfigParser;\n///\n/// struct Config {\n///     name: String,\n///     value: i32,\n/// }\n///\n/// impl Parser<&str, Config> for ConfigParser {\n///     fn parse(&self, source: &str) -> Result<Config, ParseError> {\n///         // Parse logic here\n///         Ok(Config { name: \"test\".to_string(), value: 42 })\n///     }\n/// }\n///\n/// let parser = ConfigParser;\n/// let config = parser.parse(\"name: test\\nvalue: 42\")?;\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Chaining Parsers\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n///\n/// // Parser 1: Raw string -> Intermediate representation\n/// struct FirstParser;\n/// impl Parser<&str, Intermediate> for FirstParser { /* ... */ }\n///\n/// // Parser 2: Intermediate -> Final output\n/// struct SecondParser;\n/// impl Parser<Intermediate, Output> for SecondParser { /* ... */ }\n///\n/// let first = FirstParser;\n/// let second = SecondParser;\n/// let output = second.parse(first.parse(input)?);\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Error Handling\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n///\n/// match parser.parse(source) {\n///     Ok(output) => println!(\"Parsed successfully: {:?}\", output),\n///     Err(ParseError::Syntax(msg)) => eprintln!(\"Syntax error: {}\", msg),\n///     Err(ParseError::Io(msg)) => eprintln!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n///     Err(e) => eprintln!(\"Other error: {}\", e),\n/// }\n/// ```\npub trait Parser<Input, Output> {\n    /// Parse input and return the parsed result\n    ///\n    /// This method accepts input of type `Input` and attempts to parse it\n    /// into a structured `Output` type. Returns `Ok(Output)` on success,\n    /// or `Err(ParseError)` if parsing fails.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    ///\n    /// This method will return an error if:\n    /// - The input syntax is invalid (syntax error)\n    /// - The input cannot be read (I/O error)\n    /// - The input violates constraints (validation error)\n    /// - Types don't match expectations (type mismatch)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let result = parser.parse(\"key: value\")?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse(&self, source: Input) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n\n    /// Parse input with extended options\n    ///\n    /// This method provides a way to customize parsing behavior through\n    /// options. The default implementation delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `source` - The input source to parse\n    /// * `options` - Parsing options to customize behavior\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Parsing error with context\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::{Parser, ParseOptions};\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let options = ParseOptions {\n    ///     strict_mode: true,\n    ///     preserve_comments: false,\n    ///     ..Default::default()\n    /// };\n    /// let result = parser.parse_with_options(source, options)?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_with_options(&self, source: Input, options: ParseOptions) -> Result<Output, ParseError> {\n        let _ = options; // Ignore options in default implementation\n        self.parse(source)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a file at the given path\n    ///\n    /// Convenience method that reads a file and parses its contents.\n    /// The default implementation reads the file and delegates to [`Parser::parse()`].\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `path` - Path to the file to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Successfully parsed result\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - I/O or parsing error\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    ///\n    /// This method will return an error if:\n    /// - The file cannot be read (I/O error)\n    /// - The file contents are invalid (parsing error)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    /// use std::path::Path;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let result = parser.parse_file(Path::new(\"config.yaml\"))?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Output, ParseError>\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n        Input: From<String>,\n    {\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::Io(e.to_string()))?;\n        self.parse(content.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Validate input without fully parsing\n    ///\n    /// This method performs lightweight validation to check if the input\n    /// is well-formed without constructing the full output structure.\n    /// The default implementation attempts parsing and discards the result.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `source` - The input source to validate\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Input is valid\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Input is invalid\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// if parser.validate(\"key: value\").is_ok() {\n    ///     println!(\"Input is valid!\");\n    /// }\n    /// ```\n    fn validate(&self, source: Input) -> Result<(), ParseError> {\n        self.parse(source)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get metadata about the parser\n    ///\n    /// Returns information about the parser's capabilities and configuration.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// Parser metadata including supported formats and features\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::Parser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let metadata = parser.metadata();\n    /// println!(\"Parser: {}\", metadata.name());\n    /// println!(\"Supports streaming: {}\", metadata.supports_streaming());\n    /// ```\n    fn metadata(&self) -> ParseMetadata {\n        ParseMetadata::default()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for parsers that support streaming input\n///\n/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with capabilities for processing large\n/// inputs as a stream of chunks or multiple sources.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, StreamingParser};\n///\n/// let parser = MyStreamingParser::new();\n///\n/// // Parse multiple sources\n/// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n/// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n///\n/// for result in results {\n///     println!(\"Parsed: {:?}\", result);\n/// }\n/// ```\npub trait StreamingParser<Input, Output>: Parser<Input, Output> {\n    /// Parse multiple sources in sequence\n    ///\n    /// This method processes a collection of input sources and returns\n    /// a vector of parsed results. Errors from individual sources are\n    /// collected and returned together.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let sources = vec![\"config1.yaml\", \"config2.yaml\"];\n    /// let results = parser.parse_stream(sources)?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_stream<'a, I>(\n        &self,\n        sources: I,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n    where\n        Input: 'a,\n        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>;\n\n    /// Parse sources in parallel\n    ///\n    /// This method processes multiple sources concurrently using parallel\n    /// execution. Results are collected in the order of the input iterator.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `sources` - Iterator of input sources to parse\n    /// * `parallelism` - Maximum number of concurrent parse operations\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Vec<Output>)` - All sources parsed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - One or more sources failed to parse\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// use armor::parsers::StreamingParser;\n    ///\n    /// let parser = MyParser::new();\n    /// let sources = vec![\"file1.yaml\", \"file2.yaml\", \"file3.yaml\"];\n    /// let results = parser.parse_parallel(sources, 4)?;\n    /// ```\n    fn parse_parallel<'a, I>(\n        &self,\n        sources: I,\n        parallelism: usize,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<Output>, ParseError>\n    where\n        Input: 'a,\n        I: IntoIterator<Item = Input>,\n    {\n        // Default implementation processes sequentially\n        self.parse_stream(sources)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for parsers that support incremental parsing\n///\n/// This trait extends [`Parser`] with the ability to parse input in chunks,\n/// allowing processing of large inputs without loading the entire source\n/// into memory.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::{Parser, IncrementalParser};\n///\n/// let parser = MyIncrementalParser::new();\n///\n/// // Initialize parsing\n/// parser.init_parse()?;\n///\n/// // Feed chunks incrementally\n/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"key: \")?;\n/// parser.feed_chunk(b\"value\")?;\n///\n/// // Complete parsing and get result\n/// let result = parser.finalize_parse()?;\n/// ```\npub trait IncrementalParser<Output>: Parser<Vec<u8>, Output> {\n    /// Initialize incremental parsing\n    ///\n    /// Called before feeding chunks. Sets up internal state for parsing.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Parser initialized successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Initialization failed\n    fn init_parse(&mut self) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n\n    /// Feed a chunk of input to the parser\n    ///\n    /// Processes a chunk of input without completing the parse. Can be\n    /// called multiple times before finalization.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `chunk` - Chunk of input data\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Chunk processed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Chunk processing failed\n    fn feed_chunk(&mut self, chunk: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), ParseError>;\n\n    /// Finalize parsing and return the result\n    ///\n    /// Called after all chunks have been fed. Completes the parse and\n    /// returns the final result.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(Output)` - Parsing completed successfully\n    /// * `Err(ParseError)` - Finalization or parsing failed\n    fn finalize_parse(&mut self) -> Result<Output, ParseError>;\n}\n\n/// Options for customizing parser behavior\n///\n/// This structure provides configuration options that can be passed to\n/// [`Parser::parse_with_options()`].\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ParseOptions {\n    /// Enable strict mode (disallow unknown fields, enforce types strictly)\n    pub strict_mode: bool,\n    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n    /// Allow duplicate keys (if format normally disallows them)\n    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n    /// Maximum depth for nested structures (0 = no limit)\n    pub max_depth: usize,\n    /// Custom delimiter for key-value pairs (None = use format default)\n    pub delimiter: Option<char>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseOptions {\n    /// Create new parse options with default values\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Create options for strict parsing\n    ///\n    /// Sets `strict_mode` to true and `allow_duplicates` to false.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n    ///\n    /// let options = ParseOptions::strict();\n    /// assert!(options.strict_mode);\n    /// assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_mode: true,\n            allow_duplicates: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create options for lenient parsing\n    ///\n    /// Sets `strict_mode` to false and `allow_duplicates` to true.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::ParseOptions;\n    ///\n    /// let options = ParseOptions::lenient();\n    /// assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n    /// assert!(options.allow_duplicates);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strict_mode: false,\n            allow_duplicates: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set strict mode\n    pub fn with_strict_mode(mut self, strict: bool) -> Self {\n        self.strict_mode = strict;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set comment preservation\n    pub fn with_preserve_comments(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n        self.preserve_comments = preserve;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set duplicate key allowance\n    pub fn with_allow_duplicates(mut self, allow: bool) -> Self {\n        self.allow_duplicates = allow;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set maximum nesting depth\n    pub fn with_max_depth(mut self, depth: usize) -> Self {\n        self.max_depth = depth;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set custom delimiter\n    pub fn with_delimiter(mut self, delimiter: char) -> Self {\n        self.delimiter = Some(delimiter);\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Metadata about a parser's capabilities\n///\n/// This structure describes what a parser can do, what formats it supports,\n/// and its current configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseMetadata {\n    /// Human-readable name of the parser (e.g., \"YAML Parser\", \"JSON Parser\")\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Format name this parser handles (e.g., \"YAML 1.2\", \"JSON\")\n    pub format: String,\n    /// Whether the parser supports streaming operations\n    pub supports_streaming: bool,\n    /// Whether the parser supports incremental parsing\n    pub supports_incremental: bool,\n    /// Supported file extensions (e.g., [\"yaml\", \"yml\"])\n    pub extensions: Vec<String>,\n    /// Parser version\n    pub version: String,\n}\n\nimpl ParseMetadata {\n    /// Create new parser metadata\n    pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>, format: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            name: name.into(),\n            format: format.into(),\n            supports_streaming: false,\n            supports_incremental: false,\n            extensions: Vec::new(),\n            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: indicate streaming support\n    pub fn with_streaming(mut self, supports: bool) -> Self {\n        self.supports_streaming = supports;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: indicate incremental parsing support\n    pub fn with_incremental(mut self, supports: bool) -> Self {\n        self.supports_incremental = supports;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: add supported file extension\n    pub fn with_extension(mut self, ext: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.extensions.push(ext.into());\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Builder method: set parser version\n    pub fn with_version(mut self, version: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.version = version.into();\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the parser name\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.name\n    }\n\n    /// Get the format name\n    pub fn format(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.format\n    }\n\n    /// Check if streaming is supported\n    pub fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n        self.supports_streaming\n    }\n\n    /// Check if incremental parsing is supported\n    pub fn supports_incremental(&self) -> bool {\n        self.supports_incremental\n    }\n\n    /// Get supported file extensions\n    pub fn extensions(&self) -> &[String] {\n        &self.extensions\n    }\n\n    /// Get parser version\n    pub fn version(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.version\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ParseMetadata {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            name: \"Generic Parser\".to_string(),\n            format: \"Unknown\".to_string(),\n            supports_streaming: false,\n            supports_incremental: false,\n            extensions: Vec::new(),\n            version: \"1.0.0\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Unified parse error type\n///\n/// This type wraps format-specific errors (e.g., `yaml::ParseError`)\n/// into a common type that the generic `Parser` trait can use.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum ParseError {\n    /// YAML parsing error\n    Yaml(YamlParseError),\n    /// I/O error\n    Io(String),\n    /// Validation error\n    Validation(String),\n    /// Type mismatch error\n    TypeMismatch {\n        field: String,\n        expected: String,\n        actual: String,\n    },\n    /// Syntax error\n    Syntax(String),\n    /// Other error\n    Other(String),\n}\n\nimpl ParseError {\n    /// Create a syntax error\n    pub fn syntax(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Syntax(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create an I/O error\n    pub fn io(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Io(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a validation error\n    pub fn validation(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Validation(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a type mismatch error\n    pub fn type_mismatch(field: impl Into<String>, expected: impl Into<String>, actual: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::TypeMismatch {\n            field: field.into(),\n            expected: expected.into(),\n            actual: actual.into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create an other error\n    pub fn other(msg: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::Other(msg.into())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a syntax error\n    pub fn is_syntax(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Syntax(_))\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_syntax())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an I/O error\n    pub fn is_io(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Io(_))\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_io())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a validation error\n    pub fn is_validation(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Validation(_))\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_validation())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a type mismatch error\n    pub fn is_type_mismatch(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::TypeMismatch { .. })\n            || matches!(self, Self::Yaml(e) if e.is_type_mismatch())\n    }\n\n    /// Get a brief summary of the error\n    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {\n        match self {\n            Self::Yaml(e) => e.summary(),\n            Self::Io(msg) => format!(\"I/O error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Validation(msg) => format!(\"validation error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::TypeMismatch { field, expected, actual } => {\n                format!(\"type mismatch at '{}': expected {}, got {}\", field, expected, actual)\n            }\n            Self::Syntax(msg) => format!(\"syntax error: {}\", msg),\n            Self::Other(msg) => format!(\"error: {}\", msg),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.summary())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n\nimpl From<YamlParseError> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: YamlParseError) -> Self {\n        Self::Yaml(err)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<std::io::Error> for ParseError {\n    fn from(err: std::io::Error) -> Self {\n        Self::Io(err.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_default() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::default();\n        assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(!options.preserve_comments);\n        assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n        assert_eq!(options.max_depth, 0);\n        assert!(options.delimiter.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_strict() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::strict();\n        assert!(options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_lenient() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::lenient();\n        assert!(!options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(options.allow_duplicates);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_options_builder() {\n        let options = ParseOptions::new()\n            .with_strict_mode(true)\n            .with_preserve_comments(true)\n            .with_allow_duplicates(false)\n            .with_max_depth(10)\n            .with_delimiter('=');\n\n        assert!(options.strict_mode);\n        assert!(options.preserve_comments);\n        assert!(!options.allow_duplicates);\n        assert_eq!(options.max_depth, 10);\n        assert_eq!(options.delimiter, Some('='));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_metadata_default() {\n        let metadata = ParseMetadata::default();\n        assert_eq!(metadata.name, \"Generic Parser\");\n        assert_eq!(metadata.format, \"Unknown\");\n        assert!(!metadata.supports_streaming);\n        assert!(!metadata.supports_incremental);\n        assert!(metadata.extensions.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(metadata.version, \"1.0.0\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_metadata_builder() {\n        let metadata = ParseMetadata::new(\"YAML Parser\", \"YAML 1.2\")\n            .with_streaming(true)\n            .with_incremental(false)\n            .with_extension(\"yaml\")\n            .with_extension(\"yml\")\n            .with_version(\"2.0.0\");\n\n        assert_eq!(metadata.name(), \"YAML Parser\");\n        assert_eq!(metadata.format(), \"YAML 1.2\");\n        assert!(metadata.supports_streaming());\n        assert!(!metadata.supports_incremental());\n        assert_eq!(metadata.extensions().len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(metadata.version(), \"2.0.0\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_error_creation() {\n        let syntax_err = ParseError::syntax(\"test\");\n        assert!(syntax_err.is_syntax());\n\n        let io_err = ParseError::io(\"test\");\n        assert!(io_err.is_io());\n\n        let validation_err = ParseError::validation(\"test\");\n        assert!(validation_err.is_validation());\n\n        let type_err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"int\", \"string\");\n        assert!(type_err.is_type_mismatch());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_error_summary() {\n        let err = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\");\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"syntax error\"));\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"invalid token\"));\n\n        let err = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\");\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"type mismatch\"));\n        assert!(err.summary().contains(\"port\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_error_display() {\n        let err = ParseError::validation(\"value out of range\");\n        let display = format!(\"{}\", err);\n        assert!(display.contains(\"validation error\"));\n        assert!(display.contains(\"value out of range\"));\n    }\n}\n","numLines":826,"startLine":1,"totalLines":826}}}
